name: Cerro de Gorría y Valdecasa — circular desde San Juan del Olmo
region_intro: Hiking around Avila offers a diverse landscape, from the historic city walls to the natural beauty of its surroundings. The region features rolling hills, river paths along the Adaja and Tormes, and views of the Sierra de Gredos mountains in the distance. Trails often pass through agricultural areas, small villages, and provide glimpses of ancient structures. The terrain is generally accessible, making it suitable for easy hiking. Best easy hiking trails around Avila The most popular easy hiking route is Southeastern Way of St. James and Teresian Route Loop, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete. This route combines historical paths with scenic views of the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ciudad amurallada de Ávila — circular por los Cuatro Postes, an easy 2.2 miles (3.6 km) path. This trail offers views of Avila's historic city walls and the Cuatro Postes monument. Local hikers also love the La Presa del Embalse to Castro de las Cogotas — circular, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail leading through reservoir landscapes and historical sites, often completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes. Hiking around Avila is defined by historic cityscapes, river valleys, and gentle hills. The network offers options for various ability levels, with many routes suitable for beginners and families. The routes in Avila are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Avila's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Avila?
A: Avila offers a wide selection of easy hiking trails, with over 50 routes specifically categorized as easy. These trails are perfect for beginners, families, or anyone looking for a relaxed outdoor experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes around Avila?
A: Easy hikes around Avila feature generally accessible terrain, including historic cityscapes, gentle river paths along the Adaja and Tormes, and rolling hills. You'll often find yourself walking through agricultural areas, small villages, and enjoying distant views of the Sierra de Gredos mountains. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326489267/parque-del-rio-chico-desde-el-puente-romano-del-rio-adaja-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque del río Chico desde el Puente Romano del Río Adaja — circular&lt;/a&gt; offer pleasant riverside walks.

Q: Are there any family-friendly easy hiking trails in Avila?
A: Yes, Avila has several family-friendly easy trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146102850/ciudad-amurallada-de-avila-circular-por-los-cuatro-postes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciudad amurallada de Ávila — circular por los Cuatro Postes&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, offering views of the historic city walls and the Cuatro Postes monument, making it engaging for all ages. Another good choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326397009/avila-jewish-quarter-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ávila Jewish Quarter Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which combines easy walking with historical exploration.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hiking in Avila?
A: The best time for easy hiking in Avila is generally during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, with spring offering flower-filled valleys and autumn providing comfortable temperatures for exploring the diverse landscapes without the intense summer heat or winter chill.

Q: Are there any easy circular hiking routes in Avila?
A: Yes, many easy trails in Avila are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326427602/southeastern-way-of-st-james-and-teresian-route-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Southeastern Way of St. James and Teresian Route Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which combines historical paths with scenic countryside views. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325300979/la-presa-del-embalse-to-castro-de-las-cogotas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Presa del Embalse to Castro de las Cogotas — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through reservoir landscapes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on easy hiking trails in Avila?
A: Many easy hiking trails in Avila are dog-friendly, especially those in natural areas and along river paths. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19808591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adaja River Trail loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt; are generally suitable for walks with your canine companion.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along easy hikes?
A: Easy hikes around Avila often lead to fascinating landmarks. You can explore the historic city walls and the Cuatro Postes monument on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146102850/ciudad-amurallada-de-avila-circular-por-los-cuatro-postes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciudad amurallada de Ávila — circular por los Cuatro Postes&lt;/a&gt;. Other trails might take you past ancient structures like the Dolmen del Prado de las Cruces, or offer views of significant mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2458937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Lancha&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for easy hiking trails in Avila?
A: Parking availability varies by trail. For routes within or very close to the city, such as those exploring the city walls or the Jewish Quarter, you can often find public parking lots or street parking. For trails slightly outside the city, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325300979/la-presa-del-embalse-to-castro-de-las-cogotas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Presa del Embalse to Castro de las Cogotas — circular&lt;/a&gt;, there are typically designated parking areas near the starting points.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about easy hiking in Avila?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Avila, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Hikers often praise the region's blend of historical sites and natural beauty, the well-maintained paths, and the opportunity to experience an authentic atmosphere away from large crowds. The diverse scenery, from river valleys to distant mountain views, is frequently highlighted as a favorite aspect.

Q: Are there any easy hikes that offer views of the Sierra de Gredos mountains?
A: Yes, several easy hikes around Avila provide distant views of the magnificent Sierra de Gredos mountain range. While these easy trails don't typically enter the high-altitude terrain of Gredos, they offer panoramic vistas of the mountains in the distance, especially from elevated points around the city. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ávila City Walls – Puerta del Alcázar of Ávila loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt; can offer such views on clear days.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs near easy hiking trails in Avila?
A: For trails within or close to Avila city, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and pubs to enjoy before or after your hike. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31517625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convent of Santa Teresa – Ávila City Walls loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt; are conveniently located near many establishments. For trails further afield, it's advisable to check local amenities in nearby villages or plan to bring your own refreshments.

Q: Are there any easy trails that follow a river?
A: Yes, Avila offers pleasant easy trails that follow rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19808591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adaja River Trail loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, providing a gentle walk alongside the Adaja River. These routes often feature lush vegetation and offer a tranquil experience.
tips: Small town that is located at the foot of the northern side of the Sierra de Ávila and that the center is governed by the parish church of San Juan Bautista. It is worth taking a stroll through its streets.

From this point you can see the town of Valdecasa where the mountain of El Berrocalejo rises in the background. It is an area of the Central System where batholiths appear, a granitic rock formation that forms stone balls typical of these areas.

Rounded mountain range that is easily accessible from several towns. From the geodesic vertex there are impressive front-row views of the Sierra de la Paramera, La Serrota, the Amblés valley to the south and all of La Moraña to the north.
